panels.title: Waiting Clients
panels.description: Clients queued for a server connection. Sustained >0 means pool_size is insufficient — increase pool_size or reduce query duration.

panels.title: Avg Wait Time
panels.description: Max across pools of average queue wait — adds directly to application latency. Above 50ms: check Pool Utilization and raise pool_size.

panels.title: Query p99
panels.description: 99th percentile server-side query time (excludes queue wait). Spike without QPS increase — check pg_stat_activity for locks or vacuum.

panels.title: Pool Utilization
panels.description: Active server connections / pool_size. Above 70%: anticipate saturation. Above 90%: clients are queuing.

panels.title: Memory
panels.description: Process RSS. Sudden growth without new connections usually means unbounded prepared statement cache — check Pool Cache Entries.

panels.title: Total Connections
panels.description: Current client connections (all pools). Compare with pool_size for multiplexing ratio — 100:1+ is normal in transaction mode.

panels.title: Clients by State
panels.description: Active (has server), idle (between transactions), waiting (queued). Growing 'waiting' area means pool_size is the bottleneck.

panels.title: Waiting Clients
panels.description: Waiting clients by user@database. Pinpoints which pool needs pool_size increase or query optimization.

panels.title: Avg Wait Time
panels.description: Average queue time per pool. Pool with low wait count but high wait time has slow query turnover.

panels.title: Servers by State
panels.description: Backend connections: active (executing query), idle (available for checkout). Idle approaching zero means no headroom for bursts.

panels.title: Pool Size vs Active Servers
panels.description: Active servers overlaid with pool_size ceiling. Gap between lines is spare capacity. When they converge, clients start queuing.

targets.expr: pg_doorman_pools_servers{status="active", instance=~"$instance", user=~"$user", database=~"$database"} / pg_doorman_pool_size{instance=~"$instance", user=~"$user", database=~"$database"} * 100

panels.title: Pool Utilization %
panels.description: Active/pool_size ratio over time. Sustained above 70% warrants pool_size increase; above 90% clients are already waiting.

panels.title: Query Latency Percentiles
panels.description: Server-side query time at p50/p90/p95/p99. p99 diverging from p50 — check pg_stat_activity for lock waits or long-running queries.

panels.title: Queries per Second
panels.description: Query throughput per pool. Flat QPS with rising latency signals PostgreSQL saturation. Rising QPS with stable latency is healthy growth.

panels.title: Transaction Latency Percentiles
panels.description: End-to-end transaction time including all queries and inter-query gaps. High values with low query latency indicate application-side delays between queries.

panels.title: Transactions per Second
panels.description: Transaction throughput per pool. Drop with rising latency indicates lock contention or long transactions holding server connections.

panels.title: Bytes Received
panels.description: Data rate from clients. Spikes correlate with bulk INSERTs/COPYs.

panels.title: Bytes Sent
panels.description: Data rate to clients. Large spikes indicate fat result sets — consider LIMIT if unexpected.

panels.title: Connections vs Max
panels.description: Total backend connections across all user pools vs max_db_connections. When current approaches max, coordinator evicts idle connections from lower-priority pools.

panels.title: Reserve Pool
panels.description: Reserve connections activated when all max_db_connections slots are full. Any usage means primary capacity exhausted — raise max_db_connections.

panels.title: Coordinator Events
panels.description: Evictions: idle connections reclaimed across pools (normal). Exhaustions: client errors, no connection available (critical — increase capacity).

panels.title: Inflight Creates
panels.description: Connections mid-handshake (TCP + auth + startup). Sustained high count — PostgreSQL slow to accept, check auth method or backend CPU.

panels.title: Scaling Events
panels.description: creates/s: new connections. gate_waits/s: throttled by burst limiter. fallback/s: anticipation missed, created on-demand.

panels.title: Connections by Type
panels.description: Connections by protocol. Track TLS adoption. Elevated cancel count indicates application timeouts.

panels.title: Pool Cache Entries
panels.description: Unique prepared statements per pool. Unbounded growth means dynamic statement names — fix the app or cap with prepared_statements_cache_size.

panels.title: Cache Memory (Pool + Client)
panels.description: Memory in prepared caches (pool + client). When this dominates total memory, reduce cache size or fix dynamic statement names.

targets.expr: clamp_max(sum by (user, database) (pg_doorman_servers_prepared_hits{instance=~"$instance", user=~"$user", database=~"$database"}) / clamp_min(sum by (user, database) (pg_doorman_servers_prepared_hits{instance=~"$instance", user=~"$user", database=~"$database"}) + sum by (user, database) (pg_doorman_servers_prepared_misses{instance=~"$instance", user=~"$user", database=~"$database"}), 1), 1)

panels.title: Prepared Statement Hit Ratio
panels.description: Cache hits / total lookups. Below 90%: servers frequently re-parse after multiplexing. Ensure consistent statement names.

panels.title: Auth Cache Hit Rate
panels.description: Auth lookups served from cache vs PostgreSQL query. Low rate adds latency to every new connection — increase cache_ttl.

panels.title: Auth Outcomes
panels.description: Auth success vs failure rate. Failure spike after deploy = credential mismatch. Sustained failures = check source IPs in logs.

panels.title: Dynamic Pools
panels.description: Auto-created pools for auth_query users. Unexpected growth indicates wrong database names or unplanned user sprawl.

panels.title: Process Memory
panels.description: Process RSS over time. Correlate with Total Connections and Cache Memory to isolate growth driver.

panels.title: Sockets by Type
panels.description: Open sockets by protocol. Count growing faster than connections indicates FD leak — check CLOSE_WAIT via SHOW SOCKETS.

panels.title: Primary Blacklisted
panels.description: 1 if any pool has its primary host blacklisted. Sustained = primary not recovering.

panels.title: Fallback Connections
panels.description: Connections routed to fallback hosts in the current window.

panels.title: Discovery Errors
panels.description: Failed /cluster requests (all Patroni URLs unreachable).

panels.title: Discovery Rate
panels.description: Patroni API calls, fallback connections, and errors per second. Errors without connections = all Patroni URLs or all candidates unreachable.

panels.title: Discovery Duration
panels.description: Time to fetch /cluster from Patroni API. p99 above 1s = network issues or overloaded Patroni nodes.

panels.title: Whitelist Cache Hits
panels.description: Fallback host served from cache without querying Patroni API. High rate during blacklist = cache working correctly.
